mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance above zero during rate decline periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 8.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to generate a positive mean 60-day return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during post-recession recovery phases indicates capacity utilization improvements and margin expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases during recovery periods do not correlate with positive 20-day equity returns, or the signal reverses direction in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 0.5 percent triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations when the rate threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls confirms structural labor market weakness, which depresses consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than half of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect improved household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains to generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start increases fail to predict positive 60-day equity returns, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 during late-cycle expansion phases signals supply chain bottlenecks and impending demand destruction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations when industrial production drops below the threshold in late-cycle regimes.
